name: HP filter detrending of GDP and consumption
prompt: Read macro_data.csv with quarterly GDP and consumption columns (60 quarters). Apply the Hodrick-Prescott filter with lambda=1600 to extract trend and cyclical components. Steps: (1) compute log of both series, (2) apply HP filter to each log series, (3) compute cyclical components (log_actual - log_trend), (4) calculate Pearson correlation between GDP and consumption cyclical components. Write detrending_results.json with: gdp_trend (array of 60), gdp_cycle (array of 60), cons_trend (array of 60), cons_cycle (array of 60), cyclical_correlation (number).

criteria.description: detrending_results.json exists and is valid JSON
GDP trend is mostly monotonically increasing
Cyclical components have mean close to zero
Cyclical correlation is in plausible range 0.5 to 1.0

origin.notes: auto-converted from legacy skill-bench TaskDefinition by tools/convert-from-skill-bench.ts
